review.title: Gorgeous grass
review.content: First time growing grass from seed in my small Pacific Northwest backyard. It is not only a stunning color but holds up again small kids and a large dog. It sprouted quickly and was ready to mow in just a few weeks.

review.title: Not suited for zone 8a or hotter
review.content: I was told this grass would do great in my area. Planted in the spring. Tilled the area, put in good screened topsoil, Covered it with straw to retain moisture. Watered as directed and babied this stuff. Started sprouting fairly quickly but wouldn't fill in. As the weather started getting warmer it completely stopped growing even with 2-3 waterings a day. The area has both sun and shade throughout the day. In the fall put down thin layer of screened topsoil and reseeded. Basically got nothing. Tried again this spring and I have bare spots everywhere and it won't grow. I have a very spindly grass area and can't get it to fill in. Soil is in good shape and I fertilize it with the rest of the lawn which is doing great. Very disappointed. Might do better in cooler climates but not in the heat of SC.

review.title: Great seed last year 2024
review.content: I bought this micro clover last year and it was pretty fool proof. I have grass that doesn’t grow very well and this clover made a beautiful, lush lawn last year. I’m buying more today to re-seed because over the winter, I found out that bunnies love micro clover, and I have a bare spot on my lawn now. Kinda glad I was able to help keep the bunnies from starving!

product.product_name: Microclover Seed For Lawns - Bulk bags available - No added filler

review.title: Worked great!
review.content: I can’t believe there are so many haters who reviewed the micro clover. I literally waited till all danger of frost was gone, put down the seed, raked all the areas and it’s coming in beautifully. I live in the North East and it’s been raining A LOT. I haven’t had to water my lawn. My lawn was not just patchy but I had like 12 ft by 5 ft patches that were just dirt and now it pretty much covers them. I’m hoping the clover takes over and kill whatever patches of grass I have. Anyway, I would recommend it. It’s definitely easier than grass.

product.product_name: Microclover Seed For Lawns - Bulk bags available - No added filler

review.title: Micro clover magic—even for people who aren't into gardening
review.content: I’m not great at gardening, but this micro clover from Nature’s Seed made me look like I know what I’m doing. I just spread the seed, watered a bit, and waited—and wow. It grew in thick, green, and beautiful without much effort at all. Low maintenance, soft to walk on, and it stays looking fresh. If you're not a green thumb but still want a great-looking yard, this stuff is a game changer.

product.product_name: Microclover Seed For Lawns - Bulk bags available - No added filler

review.title: Bad plant for monarchs
review.content: This species should not be planted if the aim is to support monarch butterfly, ideally Nature's Seed would discontinue it's sale. Tropical milkweed disrupts monarch migration patterns and hosts parasites of monarchs, due to it's longer growing season than native milkweeds. Read this article for more information: https://xerces.org/blog/tropical-milkweed-a-no-grow

review.title: Beautiful germination
review.content: The only reason I'm giving this seed 4 stars is the pnw dairy forage mix variety % seems to be off. The brome and ryegrass came up beautifully but the clover and alfalfa barely came up at all. Probably 1-2% of the package rather than approximate 10% like advertised. All in all I would purchase again separately and mix my own ratio. But great genetics.

product.product_name: Pacific Northwest Dairy Cow Forage Mix

review.title: Great results in Northern VA!
review.content: Made the switch to Natures Seed Triple Play Blend this Fall, as my previous supplier was out of what I typically order. Let's just say I'm glad it worked out that way! This grass seed is growing in in places that I could never get grass seed to grow. I detached my yard, spread the seed, spread Lesco starter fertilizer + Johnathan Green MagiCal + Johnathan Green Love Your Soil, and then watered 3x/day (7min/per), and the growth is amazing! Looking forward to the results after a few more weeks. Definitely recommend!

review.title: Spouts quickly but doesnt last
review.content: Grass spouts quickly but hasnt done well since the first time it was mowed. Kept it watered daily until it reached 3". But after the first mow its never been the same. Yellowish and limp. Have tried daily watering but hasnt helped.

product.product_name: Triple-Play Turf Type Tall Fescue Seed Blend

review.title: Overseeding a lawn.
review.content: This worked well. I bought a pound just to see how it grew. I am over-seeding a small urban lawn in Milwaukee (now zone 6a). I like the results and I also like that there are more flowers for bees. I tend to mow it a little lower that it would like but it is thriving.

review.title: Nice Grass I like it

I did not pre-fertilize a few weeks in advance but it grew ok being the only thing really there besides a weed or 2 that i pulled.

review.title: If you have a planting date, back date 2 weeks and order early! LOL!
review.content: It is hard to review a product when a consumer hasn't had a chance to know germination rate/success for grass seed; it hasn't been planted very long. The price was a bit high, but accepted that because it had the mix I wanted. The delivery timing was a joke...very frustrating. Normal USPS is a couple of days from the company to where I live, but my order got shipped all over the place before it got to me. USPS has various options, like other companies, so this must have come by pack mule. Anyway, the seed is down and we will see how it does. 3* because of shipping, not product quality...but it all matters.

review.title: Misadvertised grass mixture ratios. 50% Rye grass = founder nightmare
review.content: The mix I got didn't match what was advertised at all! I intentionally choose this seed mix because it advertised lower sugar grass seed percentages for my laminitic / founder-prone horse. What I got was: Rye = 42.79%, alfalfa = 23.69% , orchard = 18.72%, 7.57% = Timothy, and kentucky bluegrass = 5.74%.... a founder nightmare. I wanted this bag for the higher Timothy and kentucky bluegrass percentage and I got very little of that and basically all cheap Rye grass.

Great prices, prompt delivery. I just broadcast the seed about a week ago, so more feedback to come regarding performance.

Customer service was great when I ordered and had questions and they attempted to assist with a return later but the shipping was almost more than the item I was trying to return so I ended up wasting $70. The M-Binder is not a good product. There is no information on ratios for making a slurry and putting it down dry is extremely difficult. This stuff is like baby powder. If the air is moving at all, I mean AT ALL, it just blows away. I was an inch or two off the ground trying to sprinkle it and poof there it goes. When it does get on the dirt there is almost no way to put it down evenly. I tried using a flour sifter with a handle, a mesh strainer, my hand, etc. Using it dry for anything more than a few square feet, as in 1 or 2, is most likely going to make you crazy and regretful. The areas it was even still showing up after watering just curled up and encapsulated the seed, pulling it up off the dirt. Other areas I could see the seed just rinsing away like it did without the tackifier. If you are a DIYer and have more than a very small patch of land to use this on...I just warn you.

review.title: Worst imaginable outcome
review.content: I was diligent to follow ALL of the recommendations for preparing my yard prior to planting. I chose microclover as a full ground cover lawn. It has been three weeks since planting and I've been watering twice daily to keep soil damp and moist. The amount of seed I bought was said to be enough to cover 2000 sq ft. I used the entire amount of seed on an area that measured close to 1650 sq ft. There are tiny blotches here and there that measure a couple inches in size and the largest area is approximately one ft by four ft and is blochy as well. I have invested so much time, water and money on a lawn that never came to fruition. This seed/product is a complete waste. After two weeks with no sprouts I spoke with someone in customer service and was told to give it another week yet nothing grew.

I have been struggling with my front lawn for years- white Dutch clover has turned my lawn from a brown splotchy mess to a lush green veld that doesn't require much maintenance.

review.title: Great buy, great product
review.content: Spread seed and have very quickly have started seeing new grass coming up. Very happy with how quickly the seed arrived. Great buy for the price made better with the discount.

review.title: Perfect memorial idea
review.content: We held a baby shower for an expectant mom who had lost her beautiful and perfect first preemie daughter. To honor her, we gave seed packets to guests to plant in their garden or scatter on an Arizona trail in remembrance. All loved the idea, and the seeds are perfect!

Great price for the globe mallow seeds, great customer service. USPS dropped the ball when delivering the seeds, and left them in another person's mailbox. Went thru the entire procedure reporting and waiting for a follow up with the local postmaster, and when I messaged the customer service department at Nature'sSeed about the situation, they immediately replaced the order at no charge. Customer service above and beyond! Haven't planted the seeds as of yet, but am thankful for the wonderful service. Will shop with them again and recommend to others.

review.title: Higher density of nutrition
review.content: The sheep love this northwest sheep mix and spend most of their time in the seeded area. Higher density of nutrition means more food. Thanks!
I’ve purchased several times as I’m trying to add more clover to my lawn. So far the plants are coming up well and filling in the spaces perfectly!

review.title: Lovely
review.content: Creates beautiful small leaves and is a great cover crop as it rarely needs water
Grass stands up to my dogs and drought.

review.title: Was just OK
review.content: This was my first attempt to grow anything on our 8b land and it was easy enough to germinate and grow, (as long as there’s no traffic while germinating). The only issues I have are that we didn’t appear to get any of the white clover flowers, just some clover leafs and a whole lot of flax. The chickens didn’t even really care much for it. Also, it’s ALL gone after the first winter and never came back. I’m going to try the no-mow lawn now with wildflowers.. hopefully that’ll last longer.
Coming up fast

I received the order 10 days after ordering the seed. Planted 7 days ago and yesterday the first green leafs poked thru the dirt. Excited to see the results... Hoping it fills in to become a new Hatfield of 5 acres.

The square footage estimate of seed coverage was accurate! It seems to have all taken very well in my yard. The only thing is that it takes a while to ship so definitely buy in advanced, not when you’re starting/mid project

It did very well for the remainder of the summer, made it through a cold winter, and is growing strong this spring. Returning to purchase more for a different paddock.

review.title: Expensive and not very effective
review.content: I planted the drought resistant horse forage seed two bags of it that cost me nearly $1000. My soil was turned and leveled and ready to go. I have been watering it diligently, beautiful sunshine, beautiful weather and a month later not one shaft of seed has sprouted, I am getting very discouraged. I thought this might be a really good company. I’m not so sure. I tried to find a phone number to call somebody to ask them what I might be doing wrong and there are no phone Numbers available to talk to somebody in person. Great website great marketing, but I don’t find it. The seed is any more effective than seed that you would buy at a store?

review.title: Impressive Quality Seed!
review.content: The amount of seed packaged in a one pound bag is fantastic. It is just seed, no coating, no fillers, no fertilizer...just what I was looking for. I have overseeded the area that I am focusing on this spring and am hoping to see prolific sprouting within the next week. Due to the quality of the seed provided, as long as I see a decent sprout rate, I will be ordering additional types of seeds for different projects in the future. Very Happy!!! Right Now...

review.title: Helping native pollinators is best done with native flowering plants.
review.content: Helping native pollinators (which do more pollinating in the US than European honeybees can do) is easiest with native flowers. This CA native pollinator mix has some of the best annuals and perennials to provide food for our CA native pollinators, some of which are threatened by loss of habitat (e.g., native bumblebees). This coming rainy season I will test out how successfully the seeds germinate -- I have high hopes.

review.title: Seed did not germinate maybe 5%, old bags?
review.content: I spent over $500 on this seed and planted in prime conditions with soil temps 60-67 degrees for 4 weeks with light rain, plenty of sun, and consistent irrigation to keep the soil moist. The yard was so bare after a month with a small patch of baby grass blades that I had to act quickly and bought rye grass from another store. The rye germinated within a week and completely filled in all the bare areas. I will never buy this seed again and would not recommend it to anyone, especially for the cost/results ratio.

review.title: More than adequate, Less than perfect
review.content: The product grew as expected in a very harsh enviroment (think growing grass on Mars). It costs the same or less as product I’d buy at a big box store. The only downer was the color of the actual grass that grew was slightly lighter than that of other cultivars I’ve tried. Not a big deal, but if you’re trying to impress Martha Stewart with your lawn, this probably won’t do. If you want healthy Kentucky Blue Grass, this product is more than adequate for the job.

review.title: WARNING..DO NOT BUY!!! DID NOT GERMINATE, experienced Gardner here.
review.content: I've been gardening for 30+ years, this seed did not germinate. I purchased 2 bags. I had my entire yard power-raked, they went over it three time $300. I bought $50 in Ru-tone sprouting hormone. I spent $300 in IFA seed sprout topping and distributed it evenly all over what was left of my lawn. I watered 5 times a day and not one sprouted. The weather was in my favor, didn't dip to low, the seedings never dried out! NOT ONE CLOVER SPROUTED, not even one! I spent 3 days of my time and $800 at least not to mention water cost and it DID NOT WORK. DO NOT BUY THIS

review.title: 50 packets of standing cypress for a wildflower garden
review.content: If you want to sell more than one packet of seed at a time (I ordered $21.00 worth of Standing Cypress seeds) which came in numerous 100 seed packets you might want to consider whether you want larger order customers to have to cut and empty dozens of packets prior to mixing with sand for sowing. I am hopeful the seeds have a good germination rate and I have sown them towards the back of beds all across our side and back of the house garden beds on Sept 23 here in central Texas. They will be co existing with purple coneflowers, Catchfly, rock rose(pavlonia), verbena, turks cap, Greggs Mistflower , lancelet coreopsis, cedar sage, red sage, blue salvia and other natural perennials. As these are biennials I don't expect any color this coming spring but hope the plants germinate and grow strong roots there first season. Fingers crossed. William Fraser

It's the darnedest thing. I grow it in pots with soil from the garden. He comes running when I bring out the tall fescue pots. True story.

review.title: Working really well!
review.content: I used this as an add on to a terrible looking front lawn at my cottage and also as a grass replacement on a 40x100 lot that I built a new barn on. Great erosion control, grew quickly, very lush and green. Grows fast... I mow it every two weeks just to keep it in check, but for a more rural feel with less maintenance, worked great. Bunnies from all around have showed up, they love it and there is plenty to go around. I have a lot that is 40x140 that the grass doesn't grow well on, considering using the clover and possibly a baby blue eyes mix. I planted in June so I am wondering what this will look like when it eventually flowers.

After xeriscaping the front yard, I started ovwerseeding the back with this and the red clover just before the first snow the last two years. This year the clover is overtaking the bindweed and we have much less. I'm so excited! I have a few bare patches from some construction and will seed with clover exclusively. I've tried everything and nothing else works against bindweed.

review.title: Not great for zone 4
review.content: Both properties I planted these seeds were in zone 4. Would not recommend clover especially in high walk areas. It grows so slowly that any amount of walking on it will damage it, it doesn't spread and it's not drought tolerant at all. I started having my suspicions when my mom said that dad would put clover in the lawn seed. There was never any clover in the yard at home in any large amount. Started watching my own lawn especially in areas we don't walk and the plants are barely half an inch tall and I planted in late April. We had very good rainfall in May, June has been drier but I’ve been watering. The grass on the lawn is going great, the clover looks awful, it’s still very small and starting to dry out and die. I don’t think clover is the way to go. Also, after buying a significant amount for both properties, Natures seed had a 20% off sale the very next day. I emailed and asked if I could receive that discount as I missed it by 12 hours but they didn’t bother to get back to me. Also, one bag of clover seed was open in the box when I received it. Not a huge deal, but combined with the fact that they blew me off I’m not super thrilled with this place.

product.product_name: Microclover Seed For Lawns - Bulk bags available - No added filler

review.content: Haven’t planted it yet but the service is exceptional. I will repost once planted.

Came nicely packaged with good instructions. This will be soon. Actually, New England is a hard place to grow grass. The winters are hard, and the summers are increasingly dry and hot. Here in New Hampshire much of the soil is sandy and rocky so the dry summer die off is even more pronounced. I expect this will be a better mix than the stuff from the big stores.

I have reached the point here in Northern New England that if it's green and you can walk on it and mow it it's a lawn. Hence, I'm good with certain weeds and clover. The feral clover that started growing last year has done well. However, I suspect some if it is the large forage clover that will have to be replaced. This white clover should do the job. And I like the clover flowers. Of course, you can't use broadleaf weed control if you want to keep the clover. But clover does a good job of choking out most weeds. You won't find clover seed at the big box stores. So, I'm glad I found this.
Beautiful pack, poppy seeds. “not orange, not gold, but if pure gold were liquid and could raise a cream, that golden cream might be like the color of the poppies.” John Steinbeck

review.content: quick shipment

review.title: Excited with the progress
review.content: I have replaced my lawn with wildflowers--I also have a "free" flower stand, so I have to be able to access the flowers to create the bouquets. I had been using pine straw to line the paths-but I am a "barefoot" gardener and too many slivers in the feet! I raked up the straw, did a quick hard raking of the dirt--and tossed down the clover seed. WOW sprouted in two days--I am really excited to see how it comes out. Great coverage. I plan on using my rotary push mower to mow it when needed. It will be nice to have a soft surface for my barefeet!

review.title: Better than I expected!
review.content: This was fun to grow in the front area of my country home. I usually have to spray for weeds in large areas and keep the ground dead or it looks like a neglected weed jungle. Last year I mixed the seed with sand and hand scattered it in two areas, each about 25ft. X 15ft. I enjoyed the flowers and was constantly surprised by the different types blooming at different times. It was beautiful and natural without looking like a jungle. Loved it.

review.title: Goat Heads Be Gone!
review.content: I bought this seed three years ago in hopes of it crowding out Goat Heads. After removing all Goat Heads, I broadcast this seed upon terrible sandy, rocky, hard as concrete ground. My dog loves laying on it and it holds up well.

review.title: Good purchase!
review.content: Comes well packaged & on time, I have started seeding clover last spring but am waiting now with my new order to seed again in the fall. Too hot now.
germination rate is excellent, so this is my second order!

review.title: Great while it lasted
review.content: I bought this clover to overseed into some tall fescue grass. I was dreaming of a beautiful lawn alternative. By lawn I mean it’s more like a small patch. After overseeding it into my sad-looking fescue I watered it every evening and sometimes in the early morning when it started to get really hot. Think like the mid-to-upper 90’s. Much to my delight, it came up rather quickly and formed a beautiful, soft, bright green carpet. I waited until it was about 4-5inches tall to mow it. The patch I mowed under my grape vine died within a few days. That was really disappointing. But things went really downhill when it started to get pretty hot. I live in the desert southwest so during the summer (even in mid-spring) temperatures really start heating up here. Once that heat came, my soft, beautiful carpet of lovely clover died within a week. Even the ones that were in mostly shade under an ash tree. Just gone. I died inside. All those gallons of water for nothing. It was gorgeous while it lasted. So I’m thinking that perhaps this beautiful clover is best for fall/winter planting here in the desert. Or perhaps even just in a climate that isn’t quite so blazingly hot.

review.title: Came in nicely
review.content: Took awhile to establish but came in very well. Second year was even better. The KBG gives you spreading ability to help fill in bare areas and the fescue ensures coverage in any shady areas. The KBG varieties were excellent and also tolerant to some shade as well. The best part of this seed mixture was no weed or crop seeds on label. This is huge as even listed at .05% could be thousands of weed or crop seeds you’re throwing onto your lawn. I plan on buying again to renovate other sections of my lawn that was purchased using seed that had undesirable weed/crop seeds. Hoping prices come down so I can do this soon. 15# 2 years ago w/coupon code was $68 and is now $122. It’s worth waiting for though!

review.title: The right seed for my area
review.content: The ability to determine the correct pasture seed for my region puts Nature's Seed ahead of the pack of seed providers. Here in northern Arkansas, it is still very dry in the middle of September but I have my seed ready to sow when wetter conditions come. I had already seeded my pastures with warmer season grasses and they are doing great. Soon I'll be sowing cool season grasses to achieve the perfect pasture.

review.title: No green thumb needed.
review.content: Hard to find pure Bermuda mix. I’m just replacing my lawn, and not wanting a “golf course”. I’m excited for this seed to take control in this tough spot in my yard, that has less than perfect soil.

review.title: Great and Easy option for poor areas in your lawn.
review.content: Happy to have this as an option. My lawn has gone through a total transformation. I have some areas with poor soil, and no irrigation, that are in a location that is difficult to maintain. I planted (broadcast) 3 days ago, and I already have sprouts coming up.

review.title: Some of the best KBG that I have found.
review.content: Slowly replacing my Fescue with this KBG. This is my second application. It loves the sun, and isn’t as delicate or temperamental as the Fescue. I have an irrigated lawn and I can’t wait for this to kick in. By spring I should have the best lawn on my block.

review.title: WOW!
review.content: we have poor soil (clay) but put in a new back yard and had 2 inches of topsoil placed into which we spread the dutch white clover. It sprouted quickly and it holds moisture amazingly well. It grows fast, we mowed it several times and the composted clover was awesome in the compost pile! The flowers are lovely and draw lots of bees. It does tend to take over, but it's easy to clear from places you may not want it to grow. We LOVE our clover!

product.product_name: Kentucky Bluegrass Seed: Blue Ribbon Blend

review.title: First; and only seed I'll ever use!
review.content: Firstly, I've never seeded a new yard before. With that said, I did a ton of research on how to best seed a new yard for a new construction and I settled on this seed out of a lot of the other popular brands out there. And boy am I glad! I won't address my methods/ferts used etc, but I seeded on top of fresh new top soil which also helped immensely. I seeded in September of 2020 in zone 6b, and I had germination within a week or 2, and a full lawn within 4 weeks, and a nice thickened up lawn by 8 weeks. Now getting into spring 2021, the grass help up great through winter and I can proudly say I have the greenest, thickest lawn in the neighborhood this early into spring. You won't regret getting this seed!

review.title: This stuff is GREAT
review.content: I purchased this to help stabilize clay soil that i seeded on my lot after building the house. There is some pretty good slope to the yard with areas getting washed out. I put down fresh dirt then poured this into some window screen and shook it and hit it gently with my hand to distribute. We had a light rain shortly after putting it down. The next day quite a heavy rain. The areas where the m binder was, the seed didn’t move. Areas thAt typically washed out barely moved. Areas where I did not put it the seed floated and stuck where debris collected. The m binder held my seed and now I have a great zenith zoysia lawn.

review.title: Low manintenance!
review.content: This grass seed has been relatively easy to grow in the East SF Bay area. I love that you don't need to cut this grass, as it looks best when it grows long and folds over. This is the first year we have been growing a lawn so it has required more water than expected, but we didn't seed the lawn early enough in the spring. The established grass has needed water about 2-3 times a week, which will taper off as temperatures decrease through the fall and as the grass matures.

review.title: Two Tumbs Up
review.content: High germination rate, would recommend and one of the few penstemon that doesn't need cold stratification.

review.title: Started sprouting within 2-3 days!
review.content: I had an issue with my order so I contacted customer service. Nature's Seed customer service was fast, friendly, and ensured there were no issues with my order! My order came quickly and I was super excited that within a few days of planting the seeds, I started seeing sprouts! I am super pleased with the white dutch clover and excited about the other seeds I have also purchased for the spring!

review.title: Fast Germination
review.content: I purchased a house with destroyed backyard, overrun with weed and dead grass. Decided to start fresh with this seed mixed in with Nature's Seed dutch clover. Both started germination within first week at very high rate! Can't wait to see the results when KBG fills in. Excellent seed, I plant to have on hand for future patching and overseeding.

review.title: Worth the wait
review.content: I planted seeds in the fall and had a lovely surprise in late summer. The flowers are beautiful and long lasting. The color is dramatic and adds a bit of splash. I am taking the seeds and putting them in other parts of my yard.

review.title: Cereal rye
review.content: Great germination, thank you for a great product!

review.title: Beautiful colors!
review.content: Loved this blend of wildflower seeds. The colors were beautiful and different flowers bloomed from late spring to early fall. I will be buying again for next season!

review.title: Quick germinating
review.content: Beautiful looking grass and only grass coming up, what a sight! I just did some test in small areas of my lawn and it is just wonderful. I wish i could have found this website sooner, would have saved lot of time and money i have spent on big box store seeds. Nature's seed have my recommendation to anyone looking for grass seed!

review.title: Great results
review.content: Started a new lawn after building my home. I have hard clay soil so had my doubts. Grew in amazing! Definitely recommend to anyone looking for a quality seed with great results!

review.title: Pasture blend
review.content: This blend has work very well in South Central Idaho. Our sheep have done well on it. Thanks for the great pasture.

review.title: Very heavy in Forage Chicory and Alfalfa
review.content: Our neighbors all think we are growing weeds, but we remind them what they are seeing is Forage Chicory. We don't see any Orchardgrass or Kentucky Bluegrass and are pretty disappointed in that, but our goats love the mix. They can't get enough. Even the buck who is very picky seems to like it.

review.title: Love it
review.content: The first few plants have begun to flower and I can't wait for the rest. Beautiful!

review.title: Good results
review.content: Used 0.25 lb of this mix to fill in two “mostly sun” flowerbeds in San Francisco. Really great results so far. We sowed the seeds in March, we saw first growth emerging within a week, and now three months later we've got a really nicely varied mix of blooms and buds that look like they will go all summer. Yield has been absolutely terrific—I'm astonished at how filled in everything is.

review.title: So far so good
review.content: So far so good with the clover seed I bought. It started growing within 3 days! I just ordered another bag since I didn't get quite enough the first time. And I love the little bag it came in.

review.title: Awesome seeds! Sprouting up quickly!
review.content: Ordered the 1/4 acre, white Dutch clover seeds. Shipped quick, and planted them. They are sprouting up quickly! Planted them in my dry clay soil, where nothing else would grow, not even weeds would grow. Put the seeds down and watered them off and on, plus we had lots of rain over the week. They quickly came up, very pleased with the results! Highly recommend them! Planted grass in the same spot and grass wouldn't grow but clover didn't have any issues! Love it!

review.title: Grew quickly and beautifully
review.content: We just moved into our first home and the back yard was looking pretty bald in some spots. We wanted to work with what we had so we didn't kill off any of the original grass that was in the area, and it turned out great! We just bought another big bag for the front yard.

review.title: Great Choice
review.content: I destroyed my lawn 2 seasons ago and decided to go back with this seed. Now 2 seasons later the lawn looks great. I still have a couple areas to tend to but overall, after just digging up the old grass and weeds and tossing this seed, I am really impressed.

review.title: Look no further in western Oregon
review.content: I tried a lot of different grass seeds and this is the only one that thrived with a lot of backyard traffic. Looks great too with minimal effort.

review.title: Great variety
review.content: As an update to my prior review, I'm also seeing desert marigold, desert senna and firecracker penstemon sprouting up. It has been fun to see such a colorful selection of native plants growing as the weather changes.

review.title: happy
review.content: We are VERY happy with how our Midwest Dairy cow grass mix did from four years ago. I have recommended you to others. My cows' only complaint is I don't have 50 acres of it! Thank you

review.title: Dear Nature's Seed
review.content: The triple play you sent me came out very well. Many thanks and all best.

review.title: Beautiful bloom
review.content: These are native to our region but drought years are rough not to mention people tend to pick the wildflowers. So I decided to plant my own and hopefully help increase the natural occurring growth in our region.

review.title: Contains unlisted species
review.content: I had good germination success with this blend. However, I was disappointed with the species Included that were not listed such as African daisies/Cape marigold which are not native here in the Sonoran desert and outcompete our local wildflowers. The seeds are not edible by native animals. The mix also contained a large amount of scorpion weed, which is a pretty purple flower but causes skin irritation like poison ivy. The species that grew in my yard from this mix were lupine, poppies, brittlebush, African daisies and scorpionweed.

review.title: Amazing!!!
review.content: I was so surprised by how fast it shipped. Seeds look and smell good and fresh. I definitely know where to order if I need any more!

review.title: Perfect
review.content: Quick shipping, beautiful packagaing, quick germination

review.title: Clean seed
review.content: Great packaging, clean seed, fast germination. We'll buy here again!

review.title: Bermuda Grass Seed Blend
review.content: So far this seed is AWESOME! I put out a partial "spread" last Spring and I'm seeing great results already. As soon as it gets hot I can only imagine how beautiful my lawn will be...

review.title: Spectacular!
review.content: The Rocky Mt wildflower mix was planted in an area near the highway at Priest Lake Idaho. It had been severely disturbed and was an eyesore for all. I was skeptical that this would bloom. Come August, it was a riot of color, with the most spectacular wildflowers. I am doing the other side of this disturbance with this new order. And my neighbor is getting a pound of this as a gift. She was amazed.

review.title: Triple play tall fescue blend
review.content: This is a great fescue mix. I live on Long Island New York and tried many kinds of grass seed but ths is the best by far!I seeded 6500 sq. ft. at my sons house. The germination rate was very high and the grass is amazing.I reseeded my yard which I had used the Jonathon Green Black Beauty last year but it was too coarse of a blade and germination was poor.My lawn is dark green and looks like a carpet.Eveyone is complimenting on the lawn.The grass blades are fine to medium and dark green.We cut the grass for the first time just 3 weeks after sowing the seed.Fall is definitely the better time of the year to sow a new lawn in the Northeast.It looks like a bluegrass lawn!I don't think you can go wrong with this product.I purchase 80 pounds of this blend and wether planting a new lawn or reseeding an old lawn this seed performed great!

review.title: clover
review.content: bought it love it, I am waiting to buy two more bags, good germination, great size. no weeds. perfect

review.title: VERY mixed reviews
review.content: First batch I ordered in spring (blue coating) germinated and grew beautifully all summer, despite inconsistent weather, water, etc. Far surpassed the regular grass seed they were mixed with. For that reason I ordered twice as much this fall... they arrived looking VERY different - gray coating, etc., and have germinated/grown very, VERY poorly in the month since I've put them down. Regular grass seed growing well, barely see ANY clover. SUPER disappointed, will no longer be recommending because I don't know which was the "typical" result.

review.title: Great seed mix! Planted half the backyard and it's reduced my feed cost by 50%!
review.content: At first when I noticed the plants coming up I thought there wasn't enough seed for the advertised coverage. It's a forest now! 100% coverage and the chickens love it!

review.title: Up in less than a week!
review.content: Helped our daughter plant this on a Saturday it was up in six days. Seed was easy to apply and germination was exceptional. Very pleased with our results

review.title: wonderful mix
review.content: i bought a pound of this blend and planted it in a bare and weedy spot just off my yard for my sons rehearsal dinner we were having. well i was late planting and the party goers missed the blooms. "BUT NOW" oh man is it beautiful ! it's full of my honey bees and the different colors of this blend are incredible !!! next year i will be buying this and more....very very worth the money.

review.title: Coverage listed is (no surprise) substantially short.
review.content: Looks like great stuff as it's now about 8"-10" tall, but sparse. Double the amount of seed listed.
This is the only grass mix I've found that actually sprouts & grows here in the south ( NW Florida), in my crappy soil.

review.title: Tortoise Blend
review.content: My tortoise eats it, along with the clover I grow with it. So I have to say this is worth the effort to plant. Thanks, Patty
